items.title: Pro vs. DIY: Which Customer Segment Will Power Lowe's in 2025?
items.description: LOW leans on Pro customers with acquisitions and tools to drive bigger projects, while DIY sales face homeowner caution.

items.title: How Is Lowe's Stock Performance Compared to Other Consumer Discretionary Stocks?
items.description: Lowe's has underperformed the Consumer Discretionary industry over the past year, but analysts are moderately optimistic about the stock’s prospects.

items.title: How Will Home Depot Balance Debt, Capex and Shareholder Payouts?
items.description: HD juggles capex, dividends and debt reduction while pausing buybacks amid the integration of SRS and the pending GMS deal.

items.title: U.S. holiday spending set for steepest drop since pandemic, PwC survey shows
items.description: PwC, which polled about 4,000 U.S. consumers between June and July, said shoppers on average plan to spend about $1,552 per person, down 5.3% from last year.  The last comparable decline was in 2020, when average spend fell 7.6% to $1,187.
